page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, delivering dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. The processor architecture is x86_64, utilizing a cache hierarchy that includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench image processing scoring in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are also highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption test result. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. For language model tasks, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small models and strong text generation speeds for medium models. Multi-core scaling, database operations, and web serving throughput remain within the average range.

The absence of local NVMe storage and GPU hardware limits this instance to network-attached storage configurations and CPU-bound tasks. Additionally, the CPU lacks AVX-512 instruction support, which may impact specific vector-heavy scientific workloads. However, the dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high memory bandwidth make it qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local disk performance. It is suited for software compilation, image processing, web serving, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is an x86_64 server instance configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 16 physical cores with multithreading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores and small-model LLM text generation speeds, alongside strong single-core CPU execution and cryptographic processing. Multi-core scaling, database operations, and web serving throughput fall within the average range. Lacking AVX-512 support and local NVMe drives, this instance represents a balanced compute-to-memory profile. It is suited for CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, image processing, and text generation tasks that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a 32-vCPU server with 128 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It delivers strong single-core performance and top-tier memory scores for compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance featuring balanced memory density, strong single-core performance, and top-tier memory throughput for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated core allocation
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory performance and strong single-core CPU execution benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network storage
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and text generation workloads
